features.summary: Morgan Stanley delivered record 2021 results with $59.8B revenues and 20.2% ROTCE, driven by strong performance across all three business segments. Wealth Management added $438B in net new assets (11% growth), bringing total client assets to $4.9T. Investment Banking generated record $10.3B revenues with strong market share gains. The firm successfully integrated E*TRADE and Eaton Vance acquisitions, with most integration expenses expected in 2022.
features.management_outlook: Management raised long-term targets including 20%+ ROTCE, sub-70% efficiency ratio, 30%+ Wealth Management margins, and $10T combined client assets across Wealth and Investment Management. They expect $500M additional NII from rising rates in 2022 and $200M from fee waiver reversals. Net new asset growth is expected to remain strong but moderate from the unprecedented 11% achieved in 2021, settling in the mid-to-high single digits range.
features.risks: Key risks include potential market volatility from Federal Reserve policy changes and faster rate hikes that could impact client sentiment and trading activity. The firm faces ongoing integration execution risk with E*TRADE and Eaton Vance acquisitions. Expense pressures from wage inflation and technology investments pose margin compression risks. Uncertainty around Basel IV capital requirements and regulatory changes could impact capital allocation flexibility.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include the workplace channel with 5.6M participants and $500B+ unvested assets, improved retention rates from 21% to 24%, and cross-selling opportunities across channels. Rising interest rates provide significant NII upside with $1.3B potential from 100bp parallel shift. Strong Investment Banking pipelines and market share gains support revenue growth. The integrated platform enables better client acquisition and retention through technology investments and expanded product offerings.
